items.description: after the S3 up() migration runs, the stock table has no inventory_code column
the S3 Alembic revision module does not import app/routes or app/services; the migration stays in the repository/migration layer and never crosses the layering contract

the S3 integration test suite binds to the real Lakebase branch DB (DB_NAME=stockflow); no mock or in-memory substitute is used and the conftest fixture verifies the live connection before any S3 scenario runs
after the S3 down() migration runs, the inventory_code column is present on the stock table
after the S3 down() migration runs, every row has a non-NULL inventory_code value
a row where batch_number is NULL has its inventory_code reconstructed with an empty string in the batch segment position (e.g., 'LOC--S001')
a row where serial_number is NULL has its inventory_code reconstructed with an empty string in the serial segment position (e.g., 'LOC-B7-')
after the S3 down() migration runs, the batch_number column is STILL PRESENT (preserved; owned by the S1 split migration, not retired by S3.down)
after the S3 down() migration runs, the serial_number column is STILL PRESENT (preserved; owned by the S1 split migration, not retired by S3.down)
after the S3 down() migration runs, the inventory_code column carries a NOT NULL constraint and an attempt to insert a NULL value is rejected
